frameworks.name: affective computing
frameworks.one_line: Sensing computer user's affective state from physiological signals.

frameworks.name: pupillometry as arousal index
frameworks.one_line: Pupil diameter treated as readout of affective/arousal state.

frameworks.name: physiological signal classification
frameworks.one_line: ML classifiers discriminate stress vs relaxation from preprocessed signals.

frameworks.name: galvanic skin response
frameworks.one_line: GSR features serve as comparative baseline for PD-based sensing.

core_question: Can processed pupil diameter signals reliably classify a computer user's transition from relaxation to stress?
key_assumptions: Wavelet denoising plus Kalman filtering recovers a usable PD signal
Stress vs relaxation is a binary affective contrast
GSR provides a fair comparative physiological baseline
manipulations: Affective state induced as relaxation vs stress
Three engineered features extracted from PD signal
PD pipeline contrasted against GSR-derived features
key_findings: Average classification accuracy of 83.16% for stress identification
Highest accuracy 84.21% with Multilayer Perceptron and Naive Bayes
PD-derived features show stronger ROC discrimination than GSR features
interpretations: Pupil diameter is a powerful physiological factor for automated affective classification in HCI
